chatgpt.name: ChatGPT
chatgpt.what: Turns plain questions into writing, code, research, and images; now runs on GPT-5.5.
chatgpt.best_for: An everyday all-rounder for almost any task.
chatgpt.weak_at: Rarely the very best specialist in one lane.

claude.name: Claude
claude.what: An AI assistant that leads on writing quality, analysis, and careful reasoning; latest is Opus 4.8.
claude.best_for: Long-form writing, documents, and trustworthy answers.

claude-code.name: Claude Code
claude-code.what: A terminal agent that reads your whole codebase and ships multi-file changes; latest v2.1.176.
claude-code.best_for: Real engineering work — refactors, features, reviews.
claude-code.weak_at: Lives in the command line, not a visual editor.

gemini.name: Google Gemini
gemini.what: Google's multimodal assistant with huge context and Workspace ties; Gemini 3.5 Flash is the default.
gemini.weak_at: Quality can wobble at extreme context lengths.

cursor.name: Cursor
cursor.what: An AI-native code editor you drive with natural language.
cursor.best_for: Fast in-editor coding with full-project awareness.
cursor.weak_at: A paid IDE you commit your workflow to.

perplexity.name: Perplexity
perplexity.what: An answer engine that searches the live web and cites its sources; the Comet browser is now free.
perplexity.best_for: Quick, trustworthy research with links to check.
perplexity.weak_at: Lighter on integrations and long reports.

github-copilot.name: GitHub Copilot
github-copilot.what: AI autocomplete and chat in your editor; usage now metered in AI credits ($0.01 each).
github-copilot.best_for: Line-by-line help while you write code.
github-copilot.weak_at: Less autonomous than repo-level agents; new plan sign-ups temporarily paused.

figma.name: Figma (+ Figma Make)
figma.what: The design standard; now turns prompts into layouts and even code.
figma.best_for: Product/UI design and clean dev handoff.
figma.weak_at: Overkill for a quick one-off graphic.

midjourney.name: Midjourney
midjourney.what: Generates the best-looking images for concepts and atmosphere; V8.1 is now the default model.
midjourney.weak_at: Not wired into a production design suite.

firefly.name: Adobe Firefly
firefly.what: Commercially-safe image generation and editing inside Photoshop; Image Model 5 is now GA.
firefly.best_for: Brand-safe visuals for paid campaigns; Generative Fill.
firefly.weak_at: Best value only if you already pay for Adobe.

canva.name: Canva Magic Studio
canva.what: Easy drag-and-drop design with AI for social and marketing; now with Canva AI 2.0.
canva.best_for: Fast branded visuals with no design degree.
canva.weak_at: Less precise than pro vector tools.

veo.name: Google Veo 3.1
veo.what: Turns a prompt or image into realistic video with sound; Veo 3.1 (+ a cheaper 3.1 Lite) is current.
veo.weak_at: Less granular control than a studio tool.

runway.name: Runway
runway.what: A pro creative suite for AI video generation and editing; latest is Gen-4.5.

elevenlabs.name: ElevenLabs
elevenlabs.what: The most realistic AI voices, cloning, and dubbing.
elevenlabs.price: Free; Starter $6, Creator $11, Pro $99, Scale $299, Business $990

premiere.name: Adobe Premiere Pro
premiere.what: The pro video editor (v26.2), now with Firefly generative tools in the timeline.

davinci-resolve.name: DaVinci Resolve
davinci-resolve.what: A pro-grade video editor (Resolve 21) that's free, with strong AI tools.
davinci-resolve.best_for: Editing, color, and audio without a subscription.

claude-cowork.name: Claude Cowork
claude-cowork.what: An agentic desktop app that does knowledge work for non-developers (research preview).
claude-cowork.best_for: Delegating multi-step research, ops, and document work.

zapier.name: Zapier
zapier.what: Connects your apps and automates tasks between them, no code; unlimited Zap workflows on all plans.
zapier.best_for: The easiest glue, with the most integrations.

n8n.name: n8n
n8n.what: Open-source, self-hostable automation you fully control; per-execution billing.
n8n.best_for: Complex, code-friendly flows with no lock-in.
n8n.weak_at: More technical to set up than Zapier.

openclaw.name: OpenClaw
openclaw.what: An open-source (MIT) local AI agent that runs on your machine and acts across your apps; latest v2026.6.6.
openclaw.best_for: An always-on personal agent you own and control.
openclaw.weak_at: Powerful but risky; technical to run safely.

framer.name: Framer
framer.what: A prompt-to-website builder with strong design and animation; AI Wireframer + Workshop + AI Plugins.
framer.weak_at: Less suited to large CMS-heavy sites.

webflow.name: Webflow
webflow.what: A visual web builder with a real CMS and clean code.

v0.name: v0 by Vercel
v0.what: Generates clean React/Tailwind UI components from prompts.
v0.best_for: Turning ideas into real front-end fast.
v0.weak_at: Best for devs who'll finish the code.

relume.name: Relume
relume.what: Generates full sitemaps and wireframes from a prompt; now exports to React, HTML (Tailwind + Shadcn), Figma or Webflow.
relume.weak_at: A planning tool, not a final builder.

notebooklm.name: NotebookLM
notebooklm.what: A research assistant that answers across your own uploaded docs with citations.
notebooklm.best_for: Synthesizing your own sources with citations.
notebooklm.weak_at: Limited to what you give it.

klaviyo.name: Klaviyo
klaviyo.what: Ecommerce email and SMS built on real-time behavior; includes an AI Customer Agent.
klaviyo.best_for: Lifecycle and retention for online stores.
klaviyo.weak_at: Priced for ecommerce; overkill for simple newsletters.

anyword.name: Anyword
anyword.what: Conversion copy with predictive performance scoring.
anyword.best_for: Ad and landing copy you can test before publishing.

descript.name: Descript
descript.what: Edit video and audio by editing the transcript.

buffer.name: Buffer
buffer.what: Simple, affordable social scheduling with AI assist.
buffer.best_for: Small teams and creators across channels.
buffer.price: Free (≤3 channels); Essentials $5/mo per channel, Team $10/mo per channel

hotjar.name: Hotjar (Contentsquare)
hotjar.what: Heatmaps and session recordings that show how visitors behave; now part of Contentsquare with Sense AI.
hotjar.best_for: Finding friction on pages before a redesign.
hotjar.weak_at: An insight tool, not a testing engine; pricing now quote-gated.

make.name: Make
make.what: A visual drag-and-drop builder for multi-step automations; credits are the billing unit.
make.best_for: Complex branching flows you can see and tune.

lindy.name: Lindy
lindy.what: AI agents you build by describing the job in plain English.
lindy.weak_at: Newer; no free plan (7-day trial only).

kling.name: Kling
kling.what: A leading text/image-to-video model with smooth motion; Kling 3.0 launched 2026-02-05.

higgsfield.name: Higgsfield
higgsfield.what: An all-in-one AI video studio — generate, animate, edit, direct (Cinema Studio 3.5, Soul 2.0).
higgsfield.best_for: Creators wanting camera control in one place.
higgsfield.weak_at: More to learn than a one-click tool.

synthesia.name: Synthesia
synthesia.what: Talking-head videos with AI avatars in many languages.
synthesia.best_for: Corporate training and explainers without filming.

opusclip.name: OpusClip
opusclip.what: Turns long videos into short, captioned clips automatically.

sprout-social.name: Sprout Social
sprout-social.what: A complete social platform — publishing, listening, reporting.
sprout-social.best_for: Teams that need social data and management together.
sprout-social.price: Essentials $79, Standard $199, Professional $299, Advanced $399 (per seat, annual)

hootsuite.name: Hootsuite
hootsuite.what: Broad social management across many platforms at scale.
hootsuite.best_for: Larger teams juggling lots of channels.

jasper.name: Jasper
jasper.what: A marketing copy platform with brand voice and long-form.
jasper.best_for: On-brand blogs, campaigns, and content at scale.

vwo.name: VWO
vwo.what: A/B testing and experimentation for your site; adds VWO Copilot (free during Early Access).
vwo.weak_at: Needs real traffic to be meaningful.

optmyzr.name: Optmyzr
optmyzr.what: Cross-platform PPC management, audits, and bid automation; includes a Sidekick MCP.

madgicx.name: Madgicx
madgicx.what: Meta ads management focused on creative performance.

google-ads.name: Google Ads (Smart Bidding)
google-ads.what: Google's native ad platform with ML bidding; AI Max for Search is its fastest-growing product.
google-ads.best_for: Real-time bidding toward your CPA/ROAS goals.
google-ads.weak_at: The platform itself, not a management layer.

omnisend.name: Omnisend
omnisend.what: Email and SMS marketing built for ecommerce.
omnisend.best_for: Stores wanting email and SMS in one tool.
omnisend.weak_at: Less flexible than Klaviyo at scale.
omnisend.price: Free; Standard from $16/mo, Pro from $59/mo (scales by contacts)

gumloop.name: Gumloop
gumloop.what: A no-code builder for chaining AI steps into multi-step automations.
gumloop.best_for: Teams automating data and AI workflows without code.

pipedream.name: Pipedream
pipedream.what: Developer-first workflow automation that connects APIs with optional code steps.

activepieces.name: Activepieces
activepieces.what: An open-source (MIT) no-code automation tool, an AI-friendly Zapier alternative.

luma.name: Luma Dream Machine
luma.what: Text/image-to-video generation on the Ray 3.2 model, with AI agents.
luma.weak_at: Less fine control than a studio suite.

heygen.name: HeyGen
heygen.what: AI avatar and talking-head video generation in many languages.

final-cut-pro.name: Final Cut Pro
final-cut-pro.what: Apple's pro video editor with magnetic timeline and AI-assisted tools.
final-cut-pro.best_for: Mac editors wanting a fast one-time-buy NLE.

capcut.name: CapCut
capcut.what: An easy AI video editor built for social-first, captioned content.
capcut.best_for: Fast vertical edits with auto-captions and effects.
capcut.weak_at: Less suited to long-form professional work.

captions.name: Captions (Mirage)
captions.what: An AI video creation and editing app from Mirage, strong on talking-head clips.
captions.best_for: AI-edited shorts with auto-captions and avatars.

elicit.name: Elicit
elicit.what: An AI research assistant that searches and synthesizes across 125M+ papers.
elicit.weak_at: Built for academic research, not general use.

consensus.name: Consensus
consensus.what: A search engine for scientific research that returns cited, evidence-based answers.
consensus.best_for: Quick answers grounded in peer-reviewed papers.

grok.name: Grok
grok.what: xAI's assistant (Grok 4.3) with real-time access to X data.
grok.best_for: Current-events research and live web context.

genspark.name: Genspark
genspark.what: An AI 'Super Agent' that researches and completes multi-step tasks.

octopus-do.name: Octopus.do
octopus-do.what: A visual sitemap builder with AI generation and wireframing.
octopus-do.best_for: Mapping site structure fast and collaboratively.
octopus-do.weak_at: A planning tool, not a final builder.

uizard.name: Uizard
uizard.what: AI UI design and prototyping with Autodesigner 2.0.
uizard.best_for: Turning ideas and sketches into editable UI fast.
uizard.weak_at: Less precise than pro design tools.

visily.name: Visily
visily.what: AI-powered wireframing and UI design from prompts or screenshots.
visily.price: Free (300 AI credits); Pro $11/mo annual ($14 monthly) per editor

flowmapp.name: FlowMapp
flowmapp.what: Visual sitemaps and user flows for UX planning.

nano-banana-pro.name: Nano Banana Pro
nano-banana-pro.what: Google's Gemini 3 Pro Image model for high-fidelity image generation and editing.
nano-banana-pro.best_for: Photoreal edits and accurate in-image text.

ideogram.name: Ideogram
ideogram.what: Image generation with best-in-class text rendering; Ideogram 4.0 released 2026-06-03.
ideogram.best_for: Posters, logos, and graphics with legible text.
ideogram.weak_at: Narrower toolset than a full design suite.

recraft.name: Recraft
recraft.what: An AI design tool (Recraft V4.1) for vectors, brand styles, and consistent sets.
recraft.weak_at: Best for design assets, not photography.

flux.name: FLUX (Black Forest Labs)
flux.what: The FLUX.2 open image model family from Black Forest Labs.
flux.best_for: Developers building image generation into products.

lovable.name: Lovable
lovable.what: A prompt-to-app builder that ships full-stack web apps from a description.
lovable.weak_at: Generated code may need a dev to finish.

hostinger-horizons.name: Hostinger Horizons
hostinger-horizons.what: A prompt-to-website/app builder with hosting bundled in.
hostinger-horizons.best_for: Going from idea to a live hosted site in one place.
hostinger-horizons.weak_at: Less control than a dedicated dev stack.

openai-codex.name: OpenAI Codex
openai-codex.what: OpenAI's cloud and CLI coding agent for building and reviewing code.
openai-codex.best_for: Delegating coding tasks to an autonomous agent.

devin.name: Devin (ex-Windsurf)
devin.what: An autonomous AI software engineer; now also ships Devin Desktop (formerly Windsurf).
devin.best_for: Handing off whole tasks to an AI engineer.

bolt-new.name: Bolt.new
bolt-new.what: An in-browser AI builder that scaffolds and runs full-stack web apps.
bolt-new.best_for: Spinning up working prototypes in the browser.
bolt-new.weak_at: Token limits on the free tier.

jules.name: Jules
jules.what: Google's asynchronous cloud coding agent, built on Gemini.

brevo.name: Brevo
brevo.what: Email and SMS marketing with the Aura AI assistant.
brevo.best_for: Small businesses wanting email, SMS, and CRM together.
brevo.price: Free (300 emails/day); Starter from $9/mo, Business from $18/mo

customer-io.name: Customer.io
customer-io.what: Behavioral messaging across email, push, and SMS driven by real-time data.
customer-io.best_for: Product teams sending event-triggered lifecycle messages.
customer-io.weak_at: More technical setup than simple senders.

attentive.name: Attentive
attentive.what: Enterprise SMS and email marketing with AI campaigns and Brand Voice.

sendlane.name: Sendlane
sendlane.what: Ecommerce email and SMS marketing (acquired by Privy; in transition).
sendlane.best_for: Stores wanting unified email/SMS with reviews.

metricool.name: Metricool
metricool.what: Social scheduling and analytics with a native Metricool MCP.
metricool.best_for: Managing and measuring all channels in one tool.

publer.name: Publer
publer.what: Social scheduling across many platforms with AI assist and bulk tools.
publer.best_for: Creators and teams scheduling at volume affordably.

postiz.name: Postiz
postiz.what: An open-source (AGPL-3.0) social scheduling tool with AI assist.
postiz.best_for: Self-hosters who want to own their scheduler.

meta-advantage-plus.name: Meta Advantage+
meta-advantage-plus.what: Meta's AI ad automation suite (Advantage+) for Facebook and Instagram.
meta-advantage-plus.best_for: Automating targeting, budgets, and creative on Meta.

birch.name: Birch (Revealbot)
birch.what: Ad automation and rules across Meta, Google, Snapchat, and TikTok (formerly Revealbot).
birch.best_for: Automating bid and budget rules across channels.

adcreative-ai.name: AdCreative.ai
adcreative-ai.what: Generates conversion-focused ad creatives and copy at scale.

skai.name: Skai
skai.what: Enterprise omnichannel advertising with the Celeste AI agent.

copy-ai.name: Copy.ai
copy-ai.what: A GTM AI platform for sales and marketing copy and workflows.
copy-ai.weak_at: Broader GTM focus than pure copy.

microsoft-clarity.name: Microsoft Clarity
microsoft-clarity.what: Free heatmaps, session recordings, and Clarity Copilot insights with no traffic limits.
microsoft-clarity.best_for: Spotting page friction for free at any scale.
microsoft-clarity.weak_at: An insight tool, not a testing engine.

unbounce.name: Unbounce
unbounce.what: A landing page builder with Smart Traffic routing and Smart Copy.
unbounce.best_for: Spinning up and optimizing conversion pages.
unbounce.weak_at: Page-focused, not a full site builder.

jobs.label: Automation
jobs.why_number_one: Most integrations and the easiest start — the glue for your whole stack.

jobs.label: Video creation
jobs.why_number_one: Best all-round quality — strong prompt adherence, realism, and built-in audio.

jobs.label: Video editing
jobs.why_number_one: The professional standard, now with Firefly generative tools right in the timeline.

jobs.label: Research
jobs.why_number_one: Built for research — searches the live web and gives you cited answers you can verify fast.

jobs.label: Planning & sitemaps
jobs.why_number_one: Describe the site and it generates a full sitemap and wireframes, now exporting to React, HTML, Figma or Webflow.

jobs.label: Graphic design
jobs.why_number_one: The best raw image quality for concepts, brand visuals, and art direction.

jobs.label: Web design
jobs.why_number_one: Prompt-to-site with the best design and animation, published straight to the web with no code.

jobs.label: Web development
jobs.why_number_one: The top-ranked coding agent — reads your whole project and ships real multi-file changes.

jobs.label: Email & retention
jobs.why_number_one: Built for ecommerce — personalizes on real behavior and purchase history, the strongest for retention.

jobs.label: Social media
jobs.why_number_one: The best value for small teams — schedule, draft with AI, and reply across every channel without enterprise overhead.

jobs.label: Paid ads
jobs.why_number_one: Audits, bid automation, and budget allocation across Google, Microsoft, and more — built for people managing real ad spend.

jobs.label: Copywriting & CRO
jobs.why_number_one: Scores each variant on predictive performance, so you write conversion copy you can trust before it runs.
